mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and economic capacity, which reduces unit costs and boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change versus 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative returns in the highest INDPRO growth quintile exceed 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above its 12-month rolling average signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: In at least 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive, or the average return in triggered periods is not statistically different from non-triggered periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive monthly changes in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reinforce each other to signal broad consumer demand strength, pushing equity prices higher over the next week through improved corporate outlooks.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS month-over-month changes are positive, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in less than 55% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising but inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low, the tightening reflects strong growth expectations and is positive for equities; however, when the rate rises with high inflation, it signals restrictive policy that compresses valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
regime_hint: tightening cycle with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%)
failure_condition: In the high-inflation regime, the average 20-day forward return after a positive FEDFUNDS change is not negative, or the difference in returns between high- and low-inflation regimes is not significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A durable upward trend in retail sales (RSAFS) points to robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the near term regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS 1-month change and subsequent 5-day return is less than 0.05 over any 3-year window, or the hit rate for positive signals is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above the Fed's implicit target erodes real purchasing power and forces tighter monetary policy, which depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY>3%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 45% of the time, or the average return in high inflation periods is not significantly lower than in low inflation periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals significant labor market deterioration, reducing aggregate demand and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS decreases month-over-month and UNRATE increases, the 5-day forward return is negative in less than 55% of instances, or the average return in Jan 2000-Feb 2020 was not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are above trend but consumer spending (RSAFS) is plateauing, the economy may be in a late-cycle phase where further expansion is limited; this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the cycle turns.
failure_condition: In triggered quarters, the average 60-day forward return is not negative, or the signal fails to differentiate from non-triggered periods (Sharpe ratio difference <0.2).
